title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Go Client Systems
description: A package-signing client must distinguish protocol failure, rejected policy, malformed problem details, and an unknown write outcome. Learners cumulatively produce a Go 1.26 net/http typed response and failure classifier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
